risk.desc: Potential for overclaiming the educational display as a functional propulsion component.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to lack of detailed alignment features.

risk.mit: Ensure all cable exits have proper strain relief geometry.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Non-functional educational engine_display cartridge: combustion_chamber_dummy, injector-face visual insert, cutaway_window, slide_rail, no pressure/ignition/flight capability

physics_paths.description: Educational display only, no functional propulsion capability.
Cutaway view of engine display cartridge showing internal components.
Service access for LED lighting and visual inspection.

integration_opportunities.description: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown.
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels.
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover.
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges.
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating.

verification_focus.description: Verification focuses on mockup structure, inspection, interfaces, and unsafe overclaiming.
Interstage and module joints show locating datums, bolt-circle dummies, and alignment keys.
Assembly sequence with tool, torque, datum per step.
Dimensions match small_launch_vehicle scale: 100-500mm dia, 500-2000mm length (display model).
Ensures the display dummy can withstand static display 1g, handling 2g loads.
